political.s: Specific PAC and lobbying data for CPKC not found in search results. As a Canadian-headquartered company, CPKC maintains regulatory engagement in the US and Canada. The company lobbies on railroad industry matters and regulatory approvals.

charity.s: Kansas City Southern operates a charitable fund supporting 501(c)(3) organizations in service areas, focusing on arts, culture, education, health, and community development. The company runs the annual KCS Holiday Express, raising over $2.6 million for The Salvation Army over 21 years.
charity.focus: Community development, education, human services, arts and culture

environment.s: CPKC has released sustainability reports aligned with GRI standards and improved its Carbon Disclosure Project score. The 2023 STB merger approval included conditions to mitigate environmental impacts and anticipated carbon emission reductions. However, the company inherited legacy environmental issues including creosote contamination in the Houston area.

labor.s: The STB merger approval included conditions to protect railroad workers. CPKC reported improved employee engagement and safety metrics in sustainability updates. However, Glassdoor ratings show employee compensation satisfaction at 3.6/5 stars, and executive compensation has increased significantly post-merger while worker wages remain relatively modest.

dei.s: No specific DEI stance, programs, or metrics found in available search results. Company maintains standard corporate governance practices but DEI positions are not publicly documented.

guns.s: Not applicable - railroad transportation company does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: No comprehensive privacy policy or data protection practices documented in search results. As a transportation company, CPKC likely collects operational and customer data but specific privacy standards and protections are not publicly detailed.

execPay.s: CEO Keith Creel earned $20.1 million in 2023, with top five officers earning $63.5 million combined. Four executive vice presidents received synergy awards of $3-5 million each. CEO pay ratio significantly exceeds industry standards. Average employee salaries range from $51,715-$212,519.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
